raw_text:
CloudWeave Systems

Making cloud infrastructure observable, resilient, and autonomous

JUNIOR SITE RELIABILITY ENGINEER

San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly) · Full-time · $80,000 - $105,000 per year

CloudWeave Systems is seeking a Junior Site Reliability Engineer to join

our growing Reliability team. In this role, you will support the design,
deployment, and monitoring of our cloud infrastructure, learn incident
response practices, and contribute to improving system reliability. You'll
work alongside experienced SREs, gain hands-on experience with modern
observability tools, and help automate operational tasks. This is an
excellent opportunity to launch your SRE career in a collaborative

environment with mentorship and clear growth paths.

ABOUT US

CloudWeave Systems is a fast-growing infrastructure software company

focused on helping mid-market and enterprise organizations build and
maintain reliable, observable cloud systems. Founded in 2019, we've grown

to 85 team members and serve over 200 customers across fintech, e-
commerce, and SaaS sectors. Our platform helps engineering teams reduce
mean time to recovery (MTTR), standardize incident response, and shift

left on reliability practices.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Assist in monitoring and maintaining Kubernetes clusters in

production, including node management, workload orchestration, and
troubleshooting deployment issues

Help develop and maintain runbooks and playbooks for common
operational scenarios and incident response procedures

Contribute to the design and implementation of alerting rules in

Prometheus, with guidance from senior team members

Support the setup and configuration of Grafana dashboards for key
service metrics and SLI/SLO tracking

Participate in on-call rotations (with ramping responsibility over
first 6 months) and respond to incidents with senior SRE oversight

Write Python or Go scripts to automate routine operational tasks, such
as log processing, infrastructure provisioning, and health checks

Collaborate with development teams to instrument applications with
distributed tracing using tools like Jaeger or Datadog, and analyze

 traces to identify performance bottlenecks

Document infrastructure architecture, operational procedures, and
lessons learned from incidents to build team knowledge

Participate in SLO/SLI definition workshops and help track reliability
metrics for internal and customer-facing services

REQUIRED

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or
equivalent professional experience (0-2 years in DevOps, systems
administration, or related roles)

Strong foundational knowledge of Linux systems administration and

networking (DNS, TCP/IP, firewalls)

Experience or solid understanding of containerization concepts and
Docker

Familiarity with at least one cloud platform (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and
infrastructure-as-code principles

Proficiency in at least one scripting or programming language (Python,
Go, Bash, or similar)

Understanding of monitoring and observability fundamentals (logs,
metrics, traces)

NICE TO HAVE

Hands-on experience with Kubernetes or container orchestration

platforms

Previous exposure to Prometheus, Grafana, or similar monitoring tools

Experience with incident response processes or on-call
responsibilities

Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and tools like Jenkins, GitLab CI, or
GitHub Actions

TECH STACK

Kubernetes   Prometheus   Grafana   Jaeger (distributed tracing)   Python   Go

AWS (EC2, RDS, VPC)   Docker   Git / GitHub   PagerDuty

BENEFITS

Competitive health, dental, and vision insurance (80% employer

covered)

401(k) matching up to 4% of salary

$2,500 annual professional development and conference budget

Generous remote work policy with optional office access in San

Francisco

 Mentorship program pairing junior engineers with experienced SREs for
career growth

Paid time off: 20 days PTO + 10 company holidays

Mental health support and wellness programs

HOW TO APPLY

Apply via our careers page at careers.cloudweave.io or email resume and
cover letter to careers@cloudweave.io. Please include a brief note about
what aspects of SRE and infrastructure reliability interest you most.
Interviews include a technical screening, system design discussion, and
team panel. We review applications on a rolling basis.
